title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - Buffalo Gals
description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Buffalo Gals are a beloved Americana and old-time string band based in Nevada City, California. Known for their traditional bluegrass twang, country rags, and vibrant harmonies, the group frequently performs at local venues, farmers' markets, and community fundraisers. Margo Meredith: fiddle & vocals; Jonathan Meredith: guitar; Ann Meigs: mandolin; Michael "Banjolio" Bremer: banjo & resonator guitar; Philip Wright: Bass

title: Figurine & Character Sculpting Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Join us for an adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining! Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium to explore one’s creativity., In this fun and flexible workshop, young adults will experiment with different texturing tools, colors, and techniques to bring their ideas to life – from silly creatures and tiny treasures to action figurines and small sculptures – whatever one imagines, we will bring to fruition. This workshop is geared towards teens – with more focused guidance and instruction regarding design, details, and finishing. The students will be encouraged to exercise their creativity in this un-rushed, engaging art workshop that unfolds over 4 days. Final pieces can be painted with a variety of available mediums. We will bake our creations during the workshop so everyone goes home with a finished, cured piece.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Macrame a Plant Hanger
description: Come relax and create your own beautiful plant hanger! You will learn some of the history behind this historical art while learning basic knots and techniques using soft 100% cotton cords. Macrame is an ancient and calming hands-on art, consisting of a beautiful variety of half knot and square knot patterns. It’s as easy as tying your shoe!

title: Family Fun Day | Summer Cooldown Edition 2026
description: Offered two times a year, these are free all-day events geared toward families and introduces many young children in our community to their first-ever arts experience. We offer families the opportunity to participate in healthy art-based activities together, such as face painting, crafts, and a balloon artist and other fun entertainment., , Events & Performers, Performances by Toni Tone, Face Painting by Lucy Galbraith & Sessapaints, Balloons by Bernard Bradshaw & Timothy Roberts, Arts & Crafts with Nancy Schaefer

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 5 participants

title: The Creative Labyrinth 102: Filling the Pages
description: The Creative Labyrinth: Filling the Pages, Session Two: prerequisite Creative Labyrinth 101, Bring your handmade journal to life in this playful and inspiring follow-up workshop., Using a combination of collage, simple book arts techniques, drawing, painting, writing, and collected imagery, you’ll begin transforming the blank pages of your Australian Hinge journal into a personal creative companion., Participants will explore themes and ideas through intuitive prompts, curated materials, and creative exercises designed to spark curiosity and imagination. Whether you’re creating a nature journal, visual diary, inspiration book, sketchbook, collection of favorite quotes, or something entirely your own, you’ll leave with new pages completed and plenty of ideas for continuing your book at home., No previous art experience is necessary. This workshop emphasizes exploration, experimentation, and creative play rather than artistic perfection., What You’ll LearnSimple methods for developing themes and creative direction, Collage techniques using paper, images, text, and found materials, Basic pocket-making techniques for storing notes, sketches, and treasures, Ways to combine writing, drawing, painting, and collected imagery on the page, Ideas for building an ongoing creative journaling practice, What You’ll CreateSeveral completed journal pages, Handmade pockets and interactive elements, A personalized framework for continuing your journal beyond the workshop, Class Details, Date: July 19, Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m., Ages: Adults & teens 13+, Skill Level: Beginner-friendly, Fee: $75

title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - Big Whoop
description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., Big Whoop is a synth-pop and indie-dance band based in Nevada City and Grass Valley. Their music blends 80s electronica with power pop... very fun! The sonically unique trio consists of Anne Shisko: lead vocals, keyboards/synth; Jen Broward-Norris: bass; Jason Graham: drums.

title: Make your own Hand-Carved Wooden Spatula
description: Hand-Carved Wooden – Spoons and Spatulas (see tickets for project), 3 hours class – , There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden utensil using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece., This class is perfect for anyone curious about traditional woodworking or wanting to slow down and experience the rhythm of working with hand tools. No prior experience is necessary — just bring your curiosity and a willingness to learn., By the end of class, you’ll leave with a hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula and the foundational skills to continue carving on your own., What You’ll Create , One hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula (depending on the class ticket you choose), What You’ll Learn, • Safe and proper use of an axe, • Knife carving techniques, • How to use a scoop knife for hollowing, • Foundational greenwood carving skills, Experience Level, Beginners— No experience needed, Ages, Adults, and Teens 13+ may participate when accompanied by an adult., (The accompanying adult may choose to observe or purchase a ticket to participate.), All materials and tools are provided.

title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Saturday Get Behind the Wheel Ceramics
description: This workshop is for all levels (beginners welcome) who wish to develop and sharpen their pottery-throwing skills on the Potter’s Wheel. We will go over the basics of wedging, centering, pulling, and trimming but also cover how to throw bowls and plates. Everyone is guaranteed to leap a pot. Make that special pot that shows the sudden uptick in your skills. Enjoy playing with clay and making ceramic pottery with your own unique flair. All clay and materials included.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. title: Ceramic Exploration – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Dive into the world of clay at our Creative Clay Creations Camp! Each session, students will explore the endless possibilities of stoneware clay to create functional and decorative pottery. With handbuilding techniques your child’s imagination truly has no bounds here., Throughout the week, our young artists will learn various techniques including extruding, slab building, texturizing, and pinch pottery. Under the guidance of an experienced potter, students will use underglazes and glazes to add vibrant colors and finishes to their creations. The camp is designed to foster creativity, improve fine motor skills, and introduce the fundamentals of ceramics in a fun, supportive environment.

title: Monday Night Ceramics – Pottery Wheel & Hand building
description: Engage your mind and come play with clay in this Class! In this comprehensive Ceramics and Pottery Class, we will explore both hand-building techniques and pottery wheel skills to create functional and non-functional ceramics and pottery projects. Join us at The Curious Forge and let your creativity flourish in our supportive and inspiring environment!

title: Wonder Circle Mythic Art & Nature Journey Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: The Wonder Circle – Youth Maker XD Summer Camp, Some children see the world a little differently — they notice the light through the leaves, the shape of a shadow, the story hiding inside a stone. The Wonder Circle was made for them., Ages 9 to 12, 4 Day Camp | 3 hours a day, Monday – Thursday, 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM each day, $225, This small-group creative experience is designed for children aged 8–12 who are naturally curious, imaginative, and sensitive to the world around them. Unlike traditional art classes focused on technique or “how to draw,” The Wonder Circle invites children into a calm, story-rich environment where art, nature, and imagination work together to support creativity, emotional awareness, and confidence., Through painting, gathering natural materials, myth-inspired storytelling, and reflective creative practices, children explore their own ideas in ways that honor individuality rather than comparison. There are no right or wrong outcomes — only discovery., Each child is welcomed as a Wonderkeeper — a young creator learning to care for their imagination, their ideas, and their sense of belonging., This camp is perfect for the dreamer, the quiet observer, and the child who has always had something to say but hasn’t yet found the way to say it. Small group sizes ensure every Wonderkeeper gets the attention, space, and encouragement they deserve., Our camps are led by experienced instructors who are passionate about nurturing young creative minds. We take great care to create a welcoming, inclusive environment where every child feels valued, supported, and free to be exactly who they are., Class minimum 4 participants/ 6 maximum.

title: Tuesday Morning Ceramics – Hand-Building Techniques
description: Discover your creativity, engage your mind, and come play with clay! In this Ceramics and Pottery Class, we will use hand-building techniques to make functional and non-functional ceramics and pottery projects using slabs and coils. Clay, Glazing, and firings are included in the materials fee. Participants can sign up for additional Tuesday Morning classes each month if they wish to continue their ceramics adventure.

title: Rockin' the Gateway presents "Journey Revisited"
description: The sounds of Journey fills the stage a Western Gateway Park on August 1. If you're a fan of the legendary band Journey, then you simply can't miss Journey Revisited's electrifying live performances! Founded in 2011, this California-based band has dedicated itself to bringing back the classic Journey sound, and boy, do they deliver! With their exceptional musicianship and raw talent, they've managed to recreate the iconic sound of the album-oriented rock era, without the need for any pre-recorded backing tracks or digital vocal enhancements., These guys cover all the fan favorites, from the ever-popular Don’t Stop Believin’ and Wheel in the Sky to Any Way You Want It, Lights, and many more. But what sets Journey Revisited apart from the rest is their sheer energy and passion for the music. They pour their hearts and souls into every performance, and it shows. The audience can't help but feel the electrifying energy that fills the room, and it's an experience that stays with you long after the show is over. So, if you're in the mood for some classic rock music that will take you on a journey through time, then make sure to catch Journey Revisited in action.

title: Woodturning 101 – Craft a Beautiful Wooden Bowl
description: Are you interested in woodturning and looking to learn a new skill? In this class, you’ll learn how to turn a beautiful bowl out of wood using a lathe. The process is both rewarding and relaxing, as you take a piece of raw wood and turn it into a functional work of wood art. Under the guidance of our experienced instructor, you’ll start by learning the basics of wood lathe operation and safety. Once you’re comfortable with the equipment, we’ll move on to selecting and preparing the wood for turning. With the wood secured on the lathe, you’ll use a series of specially designed tools to shape the bowl.

title: 2D Art Explorers – Mixed Media Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Welcome to 2D Art Explorers, where young artists discover the magic of mark-making, color, and visual tricks that make your eyes do a double-take! This hands-on camp takes campers on a creative journey through four exciting disciplines — all using classic art tools and materials that artists have relied on for centuries.

description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Western Songbirds is a beloved acoustic trio based in the Nevada City and Grass Valley area, featuring singer-songwriters Sands Hall, Maggie McKaig, and Sally Ashcraft. The eclectic ensemble brings together rich three-part vocal harmonies, storytelling, and an array of acoustic instruments.

title: 2D Art Explorers – Mixed Media Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Welcome to 2D Art Explorers, where young artists discover the magic of mark-making, color, and visual tricks that make your eyes do a double-take! This hands-on camp takes campers on a creative journey through four exciting disciplines — all using classic art tools and materials that artists have relied on for centuries.

title: In the Night Dimension and Those We Meet at Twilight
description: Strange beings lurk in pixelated places, eyes peep from dark spaces, and seemingly random objects confront curious travelers. These are the dreamscapes that visitors will encounter in artist Jon Hioki’s new exhibition, In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight, opening June 20th at Wonder Docent. The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

title: Intro to Stained Glass – Level 1
description: Experience the art of stained glass in this beginner-friendly workshop! Join us to craft a small suncatcher using vibrant glass pieces in this class with Charne Morris. No experience necessary—just bring your enthusiasm!

title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: Night at the Grand Opera: A Giant Puppet Show
description: On August 11th at 7pm the Schoolhouse will have the incredible opportunity of welcoming String and Shadow's giant puppet theater to our outdoor amphitheater. The Olympia, WA theater company will join us for one night of their 2026 west coast tour, featuring their newest production ‘Night at the Grand Opera; A Giant Puppet Show'. , String and Shadow’s productions are a wonder to behold. This theater company really does it all, building larger than life sets and puppets from cardboard, fabric, and papier-mache. Their shows are written to entertain and engage all ages, and showcase a uniquely in-person art form that’s hard to come across in our increasingly digital world. You’ll love it, and you’re not going to want to miss it!, This is an ‘all or nothing’ fundraiser, meaning that if we cannot reach our goal by July 15th, the show cannot go on. , Please consider helping to back this show by purchasing ‘presale’ tickets for the event through our Kickstarter! We recommend a sliding scale donation of $20-$40 per person, which is roughly what a ticket would cost. , If you have more to give and want to ensure that the show will go on, consider a larger donation! If you can't afford a $20 donation, even $5 helps. , Got nothing to give? That’s fine! Tell your friends and loved ones and help spread the word., Once the event is funded, attendance will be ‘free’ and open to the public for everyone to enjoy.
